description_long: This is a two-part urban furniture unit consisting of a large, boxy upper section resembling a vintage CRT television or monitor, and a lower section that is a three-tiered open shelf cart on wheels. The upper section is bulky, with a deep back and a flat front screen, while the lower section features two open shelves and a flat top surface, all supported by four small caster wheels for mobility. The entire object is monochromatic and appears to be constructed primarily from metal or plastic, with a matte finish. The design is utilitarian, with no visible handles, doors, or decorative elements, and the open shelving allows for easy access and storage of items. The wheels on the lower section suggest it is intended to be easily moved, making it suitable for dynamic urban environments such as offices, schools, or community centers. The robust construction and open design make it ideal for supporting and storing electronic equipment or supplies.
description: A two-part urban furniture unit with a large, boxy upper section and a three-tiered open shelf cart on wheels, all in a matte, monochromatic finish. The design is utilitarian and mobile, suitable for supporting and storing equipment or supplies.
description_view_0: Front view showing the flat screen-like upper section and the open shelving of the lower cart.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the deep, protruding back of the upper section and the open sides of the lower cart.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back panel of the upper section and the open back of the lower cart.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the depth of the upper section and the open shelving of the lower cart.
